meta.note: subscription auth; codex reports tokens, not USD

A.missing: It omits the entering alternative and carries only movement to and proximity to a place.
A.reason: This is a generic approach schema and does not distinguish access from mere movement toward a place.

B.missing: It omits entry into the target and carries only a far-to-not-far approach.
B.reason: The movement toward a target is rendered, but the equally criterial entering component is absent.

C.missing: It omits access by approach without entry and instead requires a transition to being inside.
C.reason: This sharply explicates entering, a narrower sibling concept, rather than the full access sense.

D.missing: It lacks any near-or-inside endpoint and carries only an event involving generic movement.
D.reason: Nothing distinguishes access from arbitrary movement toward something.

E.reason: It renders an event of moving from far to near a place with resulting possibility of being inside, covering the approach-and-entry character of access.

F.missing: It lacks directed approach and required entry, carrying only movement near a place with merely possible presence inside.
F.reason: Its states can describe unrelated movement near a place, so the criterial transition of approach or entry is not secured.

G.missing: It omits entry and any directed or temporal transition, carrying only movement plus proximity.
G.reason: This generic movement-near schema fits many events other than access.
